Zia

Zia is an AI-powered capability that offers multiple features to analyze data and drive smarter decision-making.

Azure OpenAI
Integrate your Azure OpenAI subscription into EventLog Analyzer to power AI-driven summaries, contextual analysis, alert investigation, guided remediation, and more.
Enabled
OpenAI
Integrate your Azure OpenAI subscription into EventLog Analyzer to power AI-driven summaries, contextual analysis, alert investigation, guided remediation, and more.

Zia in EventLog Analyzer supports integration with multiple AI providers using the Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) approach. Both sources can be configured, but only one can be enabled at a time. All AI-powered features will use the currently enabled model.

Features
Insights
Generate instant summaries and contextual analysis across all modules. In the Alerts module, also includes verdict prediction to help classify alert intent at a glance.
Alert Triage Agent
Autonomously prioritizes the highest-risk alerts using AI, ensuring the most critical threats are addressed first.
Investigation Agent
Quickly investigate alerts with AI-powered analysis. Get guided insights and recommended actions instantly
Confirm Action

Enabling the alert triage agent improves autonomous investigation efficiency; otherwise, 10 critical alerts are investigated per hour.

Global Context

Give Zia the organisational context it needs to investigate smarter — such as known maintenance accounts, trusted IPs, authorised scanners, and expected after-hours behaviours. Context entries can be added in two ways: use Add Context to manually define an entry, or enable the Feedback option while reviewing any investigation to have Zia capture it automatically.

Filter:
Add Context Entry
No context entries yet
Add context to help Zia make smarter investigation decisions.
Runbooks AI Investigation

Define step-by-step investigation workflows that Zia executes automatically during AI Investigation. Each runbook maps to an alert type and controls exactly which data Zia queries, how it correlates findings, and what output it produces.

No runbooks yet
Add a custom runbook or import one from a YAML file.
Add Runbook
Each context is an investigation action
Import RunbookYAML · JSON · TXT
Preview
Delete Runbook

Are you sure you want to delete this runbook?

Import Context

Preview of parsed entries. Deselect any you don’t want to import, then click Import All.

Delete Context Entry

Are you sure you want to delete this context entry? Zia will no longer consider this information during investigations.

Investigation Mode
Autonomous mode investigates up to 10 alerts per hour.
Prerequisite
Alert Triage Agent Required
Evaluates and prioritizes alerts using AI before autonomous investigation runs.
Enable Alert Triage Agent to proceed with Autonomous Investigation.

Alert Name High Computation
Alert Type
Alert Description-
Alert StatusActive
Created ByAdmin
SeverityCritical
Log FormatWindows
Execution TypeIntelligent
Tags-
MITRE ATT&CK-
Criteria
Action1: actionname = “System audit policy modified” | isanomalous(Log source at an unusual Time) | isanomalous(Log source with abnormal Count) select Action1.HOSTNAME, Action1.MESSAGE, Action1.CATEGORY, Action1.CHANGES, Action1.DOMAIN, Action1.SUBCATEGORY, Action1.USERNAME, Action1.SECURITYID
Exception
Log Source Configuration
Ensure at least one device is configured under:
  • Settings > Log Source Configuration > Devices > Windows Devices
Enable Advanced Audit Policy
  1. Open gpmc.msc (domain) or gpedit.msc (standalone/local).
  2. Edit a GPO applied to Domain Controllers (domain accounts) or target machines (local accounts).
  3. Navigate: Computer Configuration > Policies > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Advanced Audit Policy Configuration > Audit Policies > Account Management.
  4. Open Audit User Account Management – select Success (optional: Failure).
  5. Apply and run gpupdate /force (or wait for normal refresh).
Note
  • Auditing may generate many logs. To manage this:
  • Open Event Viewer → Windows Logs > Security.
  • Increase Maximum log size (at least 30 minutes of data).
  • Select Overwrite events as needed to avoid overflow.
Model Training
Anomaly rules that use Machine Learning require a learning phase. It may take at least 7 days to collect and process behavioral patterns before effective detections can be made.
Mitre Framework Mitigations - Data sourced from the MITRE ATT&CK website.
Mitigation IDsMitigation NameDescription
M1042Disable or Remove Feature or ProgramRemove unnecessary and potentially abusable authentication and authorization mechanisms where possible.
M1036Account Use PoliciesUse conditional access policies to block logins from non-compliant devices or from outside defined organization IP ranges.
M1032Multi-factor AuthenticationIntegrating multi-factor authentication (MFA) as part of organizational policy can greatly reduce the risk of an adversary gaining control of valid credentials that may be used for additional tactics such as initial access, lateral movement, and collecting information.
M1030Network SegmentationConfigure access controls and firewalls to limit access to critical systems and domain controllers.
M1028Operating System ConfigurationProtect domain controllers by ensuring proper security configuration for critical servers to limit access by potentially unnecessary protocols and services, such as SMB file sharing.
M1027Password PoliciesApplications and appliances that utilize default username and password should be changed immediately after the installation, and before deployment to a production environment.
M1026Privileged Account ManagementAudit domain and local accounts as well as their permission levels routinely to look for situations that could allow an adversary to gain wide access by obtaining credentials of a privileged account.
M1025Privileged Process IntegrityEnabled features, such as Protected Process Light (PPL), for LSA.
M1022Restrict File and Directory PermissionsRestrict access to potentially sensitive files that deal with authentication and/or authorization.
M1018User Account ManagementRegularly audit user accounts for activity and deactivate or remove any that are no longer needed.
M1017User TrainingTrain users to recognize and handle suspicious email attachments. Emphasize the importance of caution when opening attachments from unknown or unexpected sources.
M1015Active Directory ConfigurationDisable legacy authentication, which does not support MFA, and require the use of modern authentication protocols instead.
M1013Application Developer GuidanceEnsure that applications do not store sensitive data or credentials insecurely.

Impossible Travel Attack High 80 AI Investigated

User corp\m.henderson (10.18.1.81) authenticated successfully from external IP 185.220.101.42 (Tor exit node, Romania) at 2026-03-24 03:12:44, and then again from internal IP 10.18.1.81 on CORP-NET at 2026-03-24 03:41:22 — a gap of only 28 minutes across geographically impossible locations. This simultaneous presence indicates active credential compromise or concurrent session hijacking. Following the anomalous logins, the account performed suspicious OAuth token generation, accessed the mailbox, and downloaded files from sensitive SharePoint directories. Classified as a True Positive — no authorized remote access or travel activity exists for this user. Immediate account suspension, token revocation, and session termination are required.

07 Jun 2017, 05:02:40
Assignee
Johnson Williams

Open

0/500
Queries Asked: 01/20
Zia
Summary

At 2026-03-24 03:12:44, user corp\m.henderson authenticated successfully from 185.220.101.42 (Romania, Tor exit node) and again from internal IP 10.18.1.81 within 28 minutes — a physical travel distance that is impossible. This was followed by confirmed Impossible Travel attack, with suspicious OAuth token generation, mailbox access, and SharePoint file downloads on sensitive directories.

Classified as a False Positive — no authorized remote access or travel activity exists. Immediate account suspension, token revocation, and session termination are required.

Insights
Zia identified 3 correlated anomalies: (1) Impossible travel — auth from Romania + internal network within 28 min. (2) OAuth token generation immediately after login. (3) Mass file access on 4 sensitive SharePoint libraries. Combined confidence: Medium. Verdict: False Positive probability 68% based on peer comparison and historical pattern.
Potential Mitigation Steps
  1. Suspend corp\m.henderson account immediately via Active Directory
  2. Revoke all active OAuth tokens and refresh tokens for this user
  3. Terminate all active sessions on CORP-NET and remote endpoints
  4. Block source IP 185.220.101.42 at firewall and WAF
  5. Audit SharePoint access logs for files downloaded in the last 2 hours
Attack Vector
An automated investigation correlated events across multiple log sources and mapped all involved entities — users, devices, IPs, and services — into a connected attack chain. The Attack vector lets you explore the full blast radius, trace how the attack progressed, and take response actions on affected entities.
Recommendations
Run Additional Playbooks
Related playbooks — Lateral Movement Containment, Pass-the-Hash Mitigation, NTLM Credential Sweep — are available for this alert. Would you like to execute them?
Tune Alerts Profile
Zia has identified potential threshold misalignment for ARP-type alerts on CORP-NET. Adjusting the alert profile may reduce noise without compromising detection coverage.
Timeline of Investigation
Alert Detected

Impossible Travel detected for corp\m.henderson with logins from a Tor IP in Romania and CORP-NET within 28 minutes, confirming a True positive. Likely credential Compromise or session hijacking; suspend the account, revoke tokens, and terminate sessions immediately.

Investigation

Zia correlated 5 alert events across 3 log sources. Found matching IOC patterns with APT29 lateral movement signature. OAuth token generation flagged as high-risk post-login action. SharePoint download volume 4× above user baseline.

Verdict: False Positive

After correlating all evidence, Zia concludes this is a False Positive with Medium confidence. The account shows no prior anomalous behaviour and the Tor IP is a known research egress node used by the IT security team. Recommend adding this IP to the allowlist after verification.

0/500
Queries Asked: 01/20
Zia
Summary

At 2026-03-24 03:12:44, user corp\m.henderson authenticated successfully from 185.220.101.42 (Romania, Tor exit node) and again from internal IP 10.18.1.81 within 28 minutes — a physical travel distance that is impossible. This was followed by confirmed Impossible Travel attack, with suspicious OAuth token generation, mailbox access, and SharePoint file downloads on sensitive directories.

Classified as a False Positive — no authorized remote access or travel activity exists. Immediate account suspension, token revocation, and session termination are required.

Insights
  • +The observed process is c:\windows\system32\certutil.exe, which is the legitimate Windows binary in its normal system location and not a copy launched from a suspicious directory such as Temp, ProgramData, Downloads, or Public.
  • +The command line shows certutil.exe -encode test.txt encoded.txt, which performs Base64 style file encoding on a local file. This can be abused by attackers, but by itself it also aligns with benign administrator, scripting, lab, or troubleshooting activity.
  • +The process tree shows powershell.exe spawning certutil.exe under the administrator account. That parent-child chain is worth attention because attackers often chain PowerShell and LOLBins, but there is no additional evidence here of download, decode, persistence, remote access, or execution of a payload.
  • +The repeated entries appear to show the same process event duplicated multiple times rather than distinct escalating actions. No supporting alert content indicates lateral movement, credential abuse, or impact on the host.
  • +With only a single security auditing source event in the alert details and no threat intelligence or malicious file reputation provided, this is best treated as a benign but correctly detected use of a dual-use utility that should still be validated against expected administrator activity on dev-agent.
Potential Mitigation Steps
  1. Suspend corp\m.henderson account immediately via Active Directory
  2. Revoke all active OAuth tokens and refresh tokens for this user
  3. Terminate all active sessions on CORP-NET and remote endpoints
  4. Block source IP 185.220.101.42 at firewall and WAF
  5. Audit SharePoint access logs for files downloaded in the last 2 hours

AWS Glue Development Endpoint ActivityDetects possible suspicious glue development endpoint activity.
New AWS Lambda Function URL Configuration CreatedDetects when a user creates a Lambda function URL configuration, which could be used to expose the function to the internet and potentially allow unauthorized access to the function's IAM role for AWS API calls.
AWS GuardDuty Critical ChangeDetects updates of the GuardDuty list of trusted IPs, perhaps to disable security alerts against malicious IPs.
AWS Key Pair Import ActivityDetects the import of SSH key pairs into AWS EC2, which may indicate an attacker attempting to gain unauthorized access to instances.
